title: Helene Small Business Recovery Act
plain_summary: This bill lets the President change rules about disaster money when state governors ask for help. Usually people cannot get money from two different federal programs for the same disaster damage. The President can now allow people to get help from multiple programs if their state governor requests it. This special rule applies to disasters that happened in 2023 or 2024 and removes income limits so more people can get help.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Helene Small Business Recovery Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Waiver of Duplication of Benefits
sections.text: In this section, the words major disaster and emergency have the same meanings as defined in section one hundred and two of the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act. The President can choose to ignore the rule that normally prevents people from getting duplicate help from the federal government. This can happen when a state governor asks for it on behalf of the state or on behalf of people, businesses, or other groups that lost money or property because of a major disaster or emergency. The President can only do this if the President decides that ignoring the rule would help the public and would not lead to wasted money, fraud, or abuse. When deciding whether to allow duplicate help, the President can think about these things. First, what the head of the Federal Emergency Management Agency recommends after talking with other federal agencies that run similar help programs. Second, whether the help that would be given is worth the cost if duplicate help is allowed. Third, whether it is fair and right. Fourth, any other public policy matters the President thinks are important. The President must decide yes or no within forty five days after getting a request to ignore the duplication rule. Even though federal law normally says loans count as duplicate help, under this new rule the President cannot say a loan is duplicate help if all federal money is being used to pay for losses from a major disaster or emergency. When using this new rule, no income limits can be used to decide if someone can get duplicate help. This section applies to help given in response to major disasters or emergencies that the President officially declared under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act that happened during calendar year twenty twenty three or twenty twenty four.
